steps.title: Identify what the geographic assumption reveals
steps.content: The fact that paleontologists assumed titanosaurs migrated south to lay eggs only makes sense if previously known sites existed in the south, not the north. The passage explicitly states there was a previous lack of known nesting sites in northern regions.

steps.title: Connect the location to the known geography
steps.content:
D directly applies the geographic logic: with no known sites in the north and scientists assuming southward migration, the northern site must be farther north than any other known site in South America.

The other three either assert temporal primacy, invent excavation complexity, or discuss organisms absent from the text.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
